title: Fleet Control Room
description: End-to-end vehicle orchestration environment with Redis, gRPC contracts, and mocked telemetry spikes.
suiteStar:
load("@babelsuite/redis", "redis")
load("@babelsuite/runtime", "service", "task", "test")

redis_cache = service.run()
telemetry_mock = service.mock(after=[redis_cache])
seed_routes = task.run(file="seed_routes.sh", image="bash:5.2", after=[redis_cache])
dispatcher_api = service.run(after=[redis_cache, seed_routes])
planner = service.run(after=[dispatcher_api])
control_room = service.run(after=[dispatcher_api, planner])
fleet_smoke = test.run(file="fleet_smoke.py", image="python:3.12", after=[control_room, telemetry_mock])

profiles.label: Local Debug
profiles.description: Browser-forwarded ports and verbose telemetry payloads.

profiles.label: Performance
profiles.description: Synthetic bursts for planner saturation tests.

profiles.label: Staging
profiles.description: Shared staging identities and realistic routing backends.

folders.name: profiles
folders.description: Driver-specific runtime knobs for local, perf, and staging lanes.

folders.name: api
folders.description: gRPC protobuf definitions and REST gateway overlays.

folders.name: mock
folders.description: Telemetry playback feeds and fault injections for route spikes.

folders.name: tasks
folders.description: Redis seeders and topology bootstrap hooks.

folders.name: tests
folders.description: Control room smoke runs and degraded GPS scenarios.

folders.name: fixtures
folders.description: Vehicle manifests and fake GPS frames.

folders.name: policies
folders.description: Route SLA validation and forbidden-zone checks.

folders.name: gateway
folders.description: Runtime-managed APISIX sidecar routes that front suite APIs and forward traffic into the BabelSuite mock engine.

sourceFiles.content:
openapi: 3.1.0
info:
  title: Fleet Control Room
  version: v1.8.0
servers:
  - url: https://fleet-control-room.mock.internal
paths:
  /telemetry/events:
    post:
      operationId: stream_telemetry
      summary: Inject mock telemetry frames into the control room pipeline.
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Successful mock response

{"vehicleId":"vh-11","speed":0,"battery":76}
{"vehicleId":"vh-12","speed":31,"battery":68}

deployment:
    role: data_plane
    role_data_plane:
        config_provider: yaml
plugins:
    - name: ext-plugin-pre-req
    - name: grpc-transcode
protos:
    - id: fleet-control-room.assign-route
      desc: api/proto/fleet_control.proto
      content: |
        syntax = "proto3";

        package fleet_control_room.v1;

        service fleet_controlService {
          rpc AssignRoute (AssignRouteRequest) returns (AssignRouteResponse);
        }

        message PingRequest {}

        message PingResponse {
          string status = 1;
        }
routes:
    - id: fleet-control-room.assign-route.grpc
      name: assign-route
      desc: Assign a route to a vehicle and publish routing metadata.
      uri: /fleet.v1.Dispatcher/AssignRoute
      methods:
        - POST
      hosts:
        - fleet-control-room.mock.internal
      plugins:
        ext-plugin-pre-req:
            allow_degradation: true
            conf:
                - name: babelsuite-resolver
                  value: '{"compatibility_url":"/mocks/grpc/fleet-control-room/dispatcher-api/assign-route","operation_id":"assign-route","protocol":"GRPC","public_path":"/fleet.v1.Dispatcher/AssignRoute","resolver_url":"/internal/mock-data/fleet-control-room/dispatcher-api/assign-route"}'
        grpc-transcode:
            method: AssignRoute
            proto_id: fleet-control-room.assign-route
            service: fleet.v1.Dispatcher
      upstream:
        type: roundrobin
        scheme: grpc
        nodes:
            ${{APISIX_GRPC_UPSTREAM_ADDR}}: 1

# Set BABELSUITE_ENGINE_ADDR to the in-agent BabelSuite engine endpoint, for example babelsuite-engine:8090.
# HTTP-family routes below keep a proxy-rewrite compatibility path so suites still run while the APISIX sidecar rolls out protocol-specific plugins.
# gRPC and Kafka surfaces now render as live APISIX routes, while MQTT/TCP/UDP surfaces render as live APISIX stream routes and shared upstream objects.
# Query parameters continue to flow to the engine unchanged so mock dispatch still happens in BabelSuite.
#
# Resolver contracts below describe the internal BabelSuite endpoint APISIX should call to fetch normalized mock data:
# - fleet-control-room.assign-route (gRPC) public=/fleet.v1.Dispatcher/AssignRoute resolver=/internal/mock-data/fleet-control-room/dispatcher-api/assign-route compatibility=/mocks/grpc/fleet-control-room/dispatcher-api/assign-route
# - fleet-control-room.stream-telemetry (gRPC) public=/telemetry/events resolver=/internal/mock-data/fleet-control-room/dispatcher-api/stream-telemetry compatibility=/mocks/grpc/fleet-control-room/dispatcher-api/stream-telemetry
#
# Transports below still need APISIX-side plugin-runner or custom sidecar wiring before they can call the resolver and emit the final protocol response:
# - fleet-control-room.stream-telemetry (gRPC) public=/telemetry/events resolver=/internal/mock-data/fleet-control-room/dispatcher-api/stream-telemetry compatibility=/mocks/grpc/fleet-control-room/dispatcher-api/stream-telemetry
#   The APISIX sidecar should terminate or normalize gRPC traffic for /telemetry/events and call /internal/mock-data/fleet-control-room/dispatcher-api/stream-telemetry to fetch normalized status, headers, and body so APISIX remains the visible responder while BabelSuite only resolves the matched exchange.
#END

apiVersion: mocks.babelsuite.io/v1alpha1
kind: MockOperation
metadata:
    metadataPath: mock/telemetry/idle.metadata.yaml
    operationId: stream-telemetry
    sourceArtifact: mock/telemetry/idle.cue
    suite: fleet-control-room
spec:
    adapter: grpc
    delayMillis: 0
    fallback: null
    parameterConstraints: []
    resolverUrl: /internal/mock-data/fleet-control-room/dispatcher-api/stream-telemetry
    runtimeUrl: /mocks/grpc/fleet-control-room/dispatcher-api/stream-telemetry
    state: null

apiVersion: mocks.babelsuite.io/v1alpha1
kind: MockOperation
metadata:
    metadataPath: mock/telemetry/spike.metadata.yaml
    operationId: assign-route
    sourceArtifact: mock/telemetry/spike.cue
    suite: fleet-control-room
spec:
    adapter: grpc
    delayMillis: 0
    fallback:
        mode: static
        examplename: ""
        proxyurl: ""
        status: "5"
        mediatype: application/json
        body: "{\n  \"code\": \"ROUTE_ASSIGNMENT_NOT_FOUND\",\n  \"message\": \"No gRPC exchange matched the incoming vehicle.\" \n}"
        headers: []
    parameterConstraints:
        - name: x-profile
          source: header
          required: false
          forward: true
          pattern: ""
    resolverUrl: /internal/mock-data/fleet-control-room/dispatcher-api/assign-route
    runtimeUrl: /mocks/grpc/fleet-control-room/dispatcher-api/assign-route
    state: null

name: Local Debug
description: Browser-forwarded ports and verbose telemetry payloads.
default: true
runtime:
  suite: fleet-control-room
  repository: localhost:5000/platform/fleet-control-room
  profileFile: local.yaml
modules:
    - redis
    - grpc
    - prometheus
observability:
  logs: structured
  traces: enabled
  metrics: enabled

name: Performance
description: Synthetic bursts for planner saturation tests.
default: false
runtime:
  suite: fleet-control-room
  repository: localhost:5000/platform/fleet-control-room
  profileFile: perf.yaml
modules:
    - redis
    - grpc
    - prometheus
observability:
  logs: structured
  traces: enabled
  metrics: enabled

name: Staging
description: Shared staging identities and realistic routing backends.
default: false
runtime:
  suite: fleet-control-room
  repository: localhost:5000/platform/fleet-control-room
  profileFile: staging.yaml
modules:
    - redis
    - grpc
    - prometheus
observability:
  logs: structured
  traces: enabled
  metrics: enabled

from pathlib import Path

SUITE_ID = "fleet-control-room"
SCRIPT_NAME = "prime_cache.py"

def main() -> None:
    print(f"running {SCRIPT_NAME} for {SUITE_ID}")
    print(Path('.').resolve())

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

echo "bootstrapping seed_routes.sh for Fleet Control Room"
echo "resolved modules: redis, grpc, prometheus"

contracts: The dispatcher API reads strict outputs from redis-cache instead of inferring connection details from container names.
Telemetry mocks are treated as first-class topology nodes so they can be selected, filtered, and replayed from the UI.
gRPC contracts in api/ remain immutable while mock payloads in mock/ evolve alongside scenario coverage.

apiSurfaces.title: Dispatcher API
apiSurfaces.description: Planner control APIs exposed through protobuf contracts with a mocked telemetry side-channel.

operations.name: fleet.v1.Dispatcher/AssignRoute
operations.summary: Assign a route to a vehicle and publish routing metadata.

mockMetadata.dispatcherRules: The APISIX sidecar should terminate or normalize gRPC traffic for /fleet.v1.Dispatcher/AssignRoute and call /internal/mock-data/fleet-control-room/dispatcher-api/assign-route to fetch normalized status, headers, and body so APISIX remains the visible responder while BabelSuite only resolves the matched exchange.

operations.name: /telemetry/events
operations.summary: Inject mock telemetry frames into the control room pipeline.

mockMetadata.dispatcherRules: The APISIX sidecar should terminate or normalize gRPC traffic for /telemetry/events and call /internal/mock-data/fleet-control-room/dispatcher-api/stream-telemetry to fetch normalized status, headers, and body so APISIX remains the visible responder while BabelSuite only resolves the matched exchange.

title: Identity Broker
description: OIDC and SAML integration sandbox with deterministic login failures, cert rotation, and secret injection paths.
suiteStar:
load("@babelsuite/postgres", "pg")
load("@babelsuite/runtime", "service", "task", "test")

broker_db = service.run()
oidc_mock = service.mock(after=[broker_db])
saml_mock = service.mock(after=[broker_db])
seed_realms = task.run(file="seed_realms.ts", image="node:22", after=[broker_db])
broker_api = service.run(after=[broker_db, oidc_mock, saml_mock, seed_realms])
session_worker = service.run(after=[broker_api])
login_smoke = test.run(file="login_smoke.py", image="python:3.12", after=[broker_api, session_worker])

profiles.label: Canary
profiles.description: New session persistence behavior and stricter cookie policies.

profiles.label: CI Smoke
profiles.description: Fast login assertions with deterministic realms.

folders.name: profiles
folders.description: Realm, issuer, and session-storage overrides by environment.

folders.name: api
folders.description: OIDC bridge OpenAPI and SAML mapping definitions.

folders.name: mock
folders.description: OIDC JWKS payloads and SAML assertion fixtures.

folders.name: tasks
folders.description: Realm seeders and certificate bootstrap helpers.

folders.name: tests
folders.description: Login smoke suites and expired-session validation.

folders.name: fixtures
folders.description: Realm definitions and claim payloads.

folders.name: policies

folders.name: gateway
folders.description: Runtime-managed APISIX sidecar routes that front suite APIs and forward traffic into the BabelSuite mock engine.

sourceFiles.content:
openapi: 3.1.0
info:
  title: Identity Broker
  version: v3.0.2
servers:
  - url: https://identity-broker.mock.internal
paths:
  /sessions/login:
    post:
      operationId: begin_login
      summary: Start an identity-broker login transaction.
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Successful mock response

realm: example
issuer: https://issuer.demo.test
seedUsers:
  - admin@babelsuite.test

deployment:
    role: data_plane
    role_data_plane:
        config_provider: yaml
plugins:
    - name: ext-plugin-pre-req
    - name: proxy-rewrite
routes:
    - id: identity-broker.begin-login
      name: begin-login
      desc: Start an identity-broker login transaction.
      uri: /sessions/login
      methods:
        - POST
      hosts:
        - identity-broker.mock.internal
      plugins:
        ext-plugin-pre-req:
            allow_degradation: true
            conf:
                - name: babelsuite-resolver
                  value: '{"compatibility_url":"/mocks/rest/identity-broker/broker-api/sessions/login","operation_id":"begin-login","protocol":"REST","public_path":"/sessions/login","resolver_url":"/internal/mock-data/identity-broker/broker-api/begin-login"}'
        proxy-rewrite:
            headers:
                set:
                    X-Babelsuite-Dispatcher: apisix
                    X-Babelsuite-Operation: begin-login
            uri: /mocks/rest/identity-broker/broker-api/sessions/login
      upstream:
        type: roundrobin
        scheme: http
        nodes:
            ${{BABELSUITE_ENGINE_ADDR}}: 1

# Set BABELSUITE_ENGINE_ADDR to the in-agent BabelSuite engine endpoint, for example babelsuite-engine:8090.
# HTTP-family routes below keep a proxy-rewrite compatibility path so suites still run while the APISIX sidecar rolls out protocol-specific plugins.
# gRPC and Kafka surfaces now render as live APISIX routes, while MQTT/TCP/UDP surfaces render as live APISIX stream routes and shared upstream objects.
# Query parameters continue to flow to the engine unchanged so mock dispatch still happens in BabelSuite.
#
# Resolver contracts below describe the internal BabelSuite endpoint APISIX should call to fetch normalized mock data:
# - identity-broker.begin-login (REST) public=/sessions/login resolver=/internal/mock-data/identity-broker/broker-api/begin-login compatibility=/mocks/rest/identity-broker/broker-api/sessions/login
#END

apiVersion: mocks.babelsuite.io/v1alpha1
kind: MockOperation
metadata:
    metadataPath: mock/oidc/jwks.metadata.yaml
    operationId: begin-login
    sourceArtifact: mock/oidc/jwks.cue
    suite: identity-broker
spec:
    adapter: rest
    delayMillis: 0
    fallback: null
    parameterConstraints: []
    resolverUrl: /internal/mock-data/identity-broker/broker-api/begin-login
    runtimeUrl: /mocks/rest/identity-broker/broker-api/sessions/login?provider=oidc
    state: null

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<mockResponse>
  <suite>identity-broker</suite>
  <artifact>mock/saml/assertion.xml</artifact>
  <status>ok</status>
</mockResponse>

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

echo "bootstrapping rotate_certs.sh for Identity Broker"
echo "resolved modules: vault, postgres"

const suiteId = "identity-broker"
const scriptName = "seed_realms.ts"

console.log(`running ${scriptName} for ${suiteId}`)

contracts: Identity providers are represented as mocks with explicit dependency edges so login flows stay observable in the topology graph.
Broker API containers consume db.url and signed mock endpoints from strict module return values instead of manual wiring.
Scenario containers treat api/ as immutable truth and keep drift isolated to mock/ and profiles/.

apiSurfaces.title: Identity Broker API
apiSurfaces.description: Login bridge APIs backed by OIDC and SAML mocks for deterministic browser and service flows.

mockMetadata.dispatcherRules: The APISIX sidecar matches POST /sessions/login and calls /internal/mock-data/identity-broker/broker-api/begin-login to fetch normalized status, headers, and body while APISIX remains the public responder.

title: Payment Suite
description: Bank-grade reference environment with Postgres, Kafka, Wiremock, and a full fraud worker topology.
suiteStar:
load("@babelsuite/postgres", "pg")
load("@babelsuite/kafka", "kafka")
load("@babelsuite/runtime", "service", "task", "test")

# Pre-registered Starlark Modules return strict structs.
db = service.run()
kafka = service.run()
stripe_mock = service.mock(after=[db])
bootstrap_topics = task.run(file="bootstrap_topics.sh", image="bash:5.2", after=[kafka])
migrations = task.run(file="migrate.py", image="python:3.12", after=[db])
payment_gateway = service.run(after=[db, stripe_mock, migrations])
fraud_worker = service.run(after=[kafka, bootstrap_topics, payment_gateway])
checkout_smoke = test.run(file="checkout_smoke.py", image="python:3.12", after=[payment_gateway, fraud_worker])

profiles.label: Local Debug
profiles.description: Verbose logs, local secrets, and relaxed timeouts.

profiles.label: Staging
profiles.description: Production-like service topology with Vault-backed references.

profiles.label: Year-End
profiles.description: Ledger rollover fixtures, end-of-year reporting toggles, and settlement edge cases.

folders.name: profiles
folders.description: Environment variable toggles and runtime overrides.

folders.name: api
folders.description: Immutable OpenAPI and protobuf contracts for the suite.

folders.name: mock
folders.description: Wiremock mappings and scenario-specific stub bodies.

folders.name: tasks
folders.description: Boot-time migrations and broker preparation scripts.

folders.name: tests
folders.description: Smoke tests and attack-path executions.

folders.name: fixtures
folders.description: Static input data for cards, merchants, and seeded accounts.

folders.name: policies
folders.description: Rego payload validation and ledger invariants.

folders.name: gateway
folders.description: Runtime-managed APISIX sidecar routes that front suite APIs and forward traffic into the BabelSuite mock engine.

sourceFiles.content:
openapi: 3.1.0
info:
  title: Payment Suite
  version: v2.4.1
servers:
  - url: https://payment-suite.mock.internal
paths:
  /payments:
    post:
      operationId: create_payment
      summary: Create a payment authorization and fan out to Stripe.
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Successful mock response
  /payments/{paymentId}:
    get:
      operationId: get_payment
      summary: Retrieve a previously authorized payment and ledger status.
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Successful mock response

id,name,status
m-117,Core Merchant,active
m-441,Risky Merchant,review

deployment:
    role: data_plane
    role_data_plane:
        config_provider: yaml
plugins:
    - name: ext-plugin-pre-req
    - name: proxy-rewrite
routes:
    - id: payment-suite.create-payment
      name: create-payment
      desc: Create a payment authorization and fan out to Stripe.
      uri: /payments
      methods:
        - POST
      hosts:
        - payment-suite.mock.internal
      plugins:
        ext-plugin-pre-req:
            allow_degradation: true
            conf:
                - name: babelsuite-resolver
                  value: '{"compatibility_url":"/mocks/rest/payment-suite/payment-gateway/payments","operation_id":"create-payment","protocol":"REST","public_path":"/payments","resolver_url":"/internal/mock-data/payment-suite/payment-gateway/create-payment"}'
        proxy-rewrite:
            headers:
                set:
                    X-Babelsuite-Dispatcher: apisix
                    X-Babelsuite-Operation: create-payment
            uri: /mocks/rest/payment-suite/payment-gateway/payments
      upstream:
        type: roundrobin
        scheme: http
        nodes:
            ${{BABELSUITE_ENGINE_ADDR}}: 1
    - id: payment-suite.get-payment
      name: get-payment
      desc: Retrieve a previously authorized payment and ledger status.
      uri: /payments/*
      methods:
        - GET
      hosts:
        - payment-suite.mock.internal
      plugins:
        ext-plugin-pre-req:
            allow_degradation: true
            conf:
                - name: babelsuite-resolver
                  value: '{"compatibility_url":"/mocks/rest/payment-suite/payment-gateway/payments/pay_1043","operation_id":"get-payment","protocol":"REST","public_path":"/payments/{paymentId}","resolver_url":"/internal/mock-data/payment-suite/payment-gateway/get-payment"}'
        proxy-rewrite:
            headers:
                set:
                    X-Babelsuite-Dispatcher: apisix
                    X-Babelsuite-Operation: get-payment
            regex_uri:
                - ^/payments/([^/]+)$
                - /mocks/rest/payment-suite/payment-gateway/payments/$1
      upstream:
        type: roundrobin
        scheme: http
        nodes:
            ${{BABELSUITE_ENGINE_ADDR}}: 1

# Set BABELSUITE_ENGINE_ADDR to the in-agent BabelSuite engine endpoint, for example babelsuite-engine:8090.
# HTTP-family routes below keep a proxy-rewrite compatibility path so suites still run while the APISIX sidecar rolls out protocol-specific plugins.
# gRPC and Kafka surfaces now render as live APISIX routes, while MQTT/TCP/UDP surfaces render as live APISIX stream routes and shared upstream objects.
# Query parameters continue to flow to the engine unchanged so mock dispatch still happens in BabelSuite.
#
# Resolver contracts below describe the internal BabelSuite endpoint APISIX should call to fetch normalized mock data:
# - payment-suite.create-payment (REST) public=/payments resolver=/internal/mock-data/payment-suite/payment-gateway/create-payment compatibility=/mocks/rest/payment-suite/payment-gateway/payments
# - payment-suite.get-payment (REST) public=/payments/{paymentId} resolver=/internal/mock-data/payment-suite/payment-gateway/get-payment compatibility=/mocks/rest/payment-suite/payment-gateway/payments/pay_1043
#END

apiVersion: mocks.babelsuite.io/v1alpha1
kind: MockOperation
metadata:
    metadataPath: mock/wiremock/stripe/create-charge.metadata.yaml
    operationId: create-payment
    sourceArtifact: mock/wiremock/stripe/create-charge.cue
    suite: payment-suite
spec:
    adapter: rest
    delayMillis: 80
    fallback:
        mode: static
        examplename: ""
        proxyurl: ""
        status: "422"
        mediatype: application/json
        body: |-
            {
              "code": "UNKNOWN_PAYMENT_SCENARIO",
              "message": "No payment mock matched the incoming scenario."
            }
        headers:
            - name: x-mock-fallback
              value: payment-create
    parameterConstraints:
        - name: status
          source: query
          required: true
          forward: false
          pattern: ^(approved|review)$
        - name: content-type
          source: header
          required: true
          forward: false
          pattern: (?i)^application/json
        - name: x-suite-profile
          source: header
          required: false
          forward: true
          pattern: ""
    resolverUrl: /internal/mock-data/payment-suite/payment-gateway/create-payment
    runtimeUrl: /mocks/rest/payment-suite/payment-gateway/payments?status=approved
    state:
        lookupkeytemplate: ""
        mutationkeytemplate: payment:{{ response.body.paymentId }}
        defaults:
            ledgerState: pending
            status: created
        transitions:
            - onexample: approved-card
              mutationkeytemplate: ""
              set:
                ledgerState: posted
                profile: '{{ request.headers.x-suite-profile }}'
                status: authorized
              delete: []
              increment: {}
            - onexample: fraud-review
              mutationkeytemplate: ""
              set:
                fraudScore: '{{ request.headers.x-fraud-score }}'
                ledgerState: held
                profile: '{{ request.headers.x-suite-profile }}'
                status: pending_review
              delete: []
              increment: {}

apiVersion: mocks.babelsuite.io/v1alpha1
kind: MockOperation
metadata:
    metadataPath: mock/wiremock/stripe/get-payment.metadata.yaml
    operationId: get-payment
    sourceArtifact: mock/wiremock/stripe/get-payment.cue
    suite: payment-suite
spec:
    adapter: rest
    delayMillis: 0
    fallback:
        mode: static
        examplename: ""
        proxyurl: ""
        status: "404"
        mediatype: application/json
        body: |-
            {
              "code": "PAYMENT_NOT_FOUND",
              "message": "No payment state exists for {{ request.path.paymentId }}."
            }
        headers: []
    parameterConstraints:
        - name: paymentId
          source: path
          required: true
          forward: false
          pattern: ^pay_[A-Za-z0-9]+$
        - name: x-request-id
          source: header
          required: false
          forward: true
          pattern: ""
    resolverUrl: /internal/mock-data/payment-suite/payment-gateway/get-payment
    runtimeUrl: /mocks/rest/payment-suite/payment-gateway/payments/pay_1043
    state:
        lookupkeytemplate: payment:{{ request.path.paymentId }}
        mutationkeytemplate: ""
        defaults:
            ledgerState: posted
            profile: local.yaml
            status: authorized
        transitions: []

// Code generated by BabelSuite. DO NOT EDIT.
suite: "payment-suite"
artifact: "mock/wiremock/stripe/refund.cue"
message: "No seeded exchanges matched this mock file, so BabelSuite generated a placeholder preview."

name: Local Debug
description: Verbose logs, local secrets, and relaxed timeouts.
default: true
runtime:
  suite: payment-suite
  repository: localhost:5000/core-platform/payment-suite
  profileFile: local.yaml
modules:
    - postgres
    - kafka
observability:
  logs: structured
  traces: enabled
  metrics: enabled

name: Staging
description: Production-like service topology with Vault-backed references.
default: false
runtime:
  suite: payment-suite
  repository: localhost:5000/core-platform/payment-suite
  profileFile: staging.yaml
modules:
    - postgres
    - kafka
observability:
  logs: structured
  traces: enabled
  metrics: enabled

name: Year-End
description: Ledger rollover fixtures, end-of-year reporting toggles, and settlement edge cases.
default: false
runtime:
  suite: payment-suite
  repository: localhost:5000/core-platform/payment-suite
  profileFile: year.yaml
modules:
    - postgres
    - kafka
observability:
  logs: structured
  traces: enabled
  metrics: enabled

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

echo "bootstrapping bootstrap_topics.sh for Payment Suite"
echo "resolved modules: postgres, kafka"

from pathlib import Path

SUITE_ID = "payment-suite"
SCRIPT_NAME = "migrate.py"

def main() -> None:
    print(f"running {SCRIPT_NAME} for {SUITE_ID}")
    print(Path('.').resolve())

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

contracts: Use load("@babelsuite/postgres", "pg") to provision the database and read db.url from the returned struct.
Kafka topics are exposed through a strict address contract so scenario containers never hand-craft broker URLs.
Mocks live under mock/ and are selected through dispatch criteria rather than ad-hoc conditionals inside suite.star.

apiSurfaces.title: Payment Gateway
apiSurfaces.description: Public checkout APIs backed by Wiremock-friendly examples and deterministic fraud scores.

operations.name: /payments
operations.summary: Create a payment authorization and fan out to Stripe.

mockMetadata.dispatcherRules: The APISIX sidecar matches POST /payments and calls /internal/mock-data/payment-suite/payment-gateway/create-payment to fetch normalized status, headers, and body while APISIX remains the public responder.

operations.name: /payments/{paymentId}
operations.summary: Retrieve a previously authorized payment and ledger status.

mockMetadata.dispatcherRules: The APISIX sidecar matches GET /payments/{paymentId} and calls /internal/mock-data/payment-suite/payment-gateway/get-payment to fetch normalized status, headers, and body while APISIX remains the public responder.

title: Returns Control Plane
description: Reverse-logistics suite that shows BabelSuite's richer mock runtime with split metadata, templating, constraints, fallback, state, and multi-protocol surfaces.
suiteStar:
load("@babelsuite/kafka", "kafka")
load("@babelsuite/postgres", "pg")
load("@babelsuite/runtime", "service", "task", "test")

returns_db = service.run()
broker = service.run()
refunds_mock = service.mock(after=[returns_db])
pricing_mock = service.mock(after=[returns_db])
events_mock = service.mock(after=[broker])
seed_topics = task.run(file="seed_topics.sh", image="bash:5.2", after=[broker])
seed_routes = task.run(file="seed_routes.ts", image="node:22", after=[returns_db, refunds_mock, pricing_mock])
returns_api = service.run(after=[returns_db, refunds_mock, pricing_mock, seed_routes])
refund_worker = service.run(after=[broker, returns_api, events_mock, seed_topics])
returns_smoke = test.run(file="returns_smoke.py", image="python:3.12", after=[returns_api, refund_worker])

profiles.label: Local Debug
profiles.description: Verbose logs, seeded return ids, and open worker traces.

profiles.label: Canary Routing
profiles.description: Canary refund rules with manual-review escalation and delayed callbacks.

profiles.label: Peak Season
profiles.description: High-volume return traffic with fraud screening and worker backpressure.

folders.name: profiles
folders.description: Launch profiles for local, canary, and peak-season refund traffic.

folders.name: api
folders.description: OpenAPI and protobuf contracts for returns and refund pricing.

folders.name: mock
folders.description: Mock payloads plus metadata that control dispatch, fallback, and state.

folders.name: tasks
folders.description: Bootstrap hooks for Kafka topics and refund routing tables.

folders.name: tests
folders.description: Smoke and manual-review scenarios for reverse logistics flows.

folders.name: fixtures
folders.description: Seeded return cases and customer profiles.

folders.name: policies
folders.description: Refund-limit and event-schema validation policies.

folders.name: gateway
folders.description: Runtime-managed APISIX sidecar routes that front suite APIs and forward traffic into the BabelSuite mock engine.

sourceFiles.content:
openapi: 3.1.0
info:
  title: Returns Control Plane
  version: v1.0.0
servers:
  - url: https://returns-control-plane.mock.internal
paths:
  /returns:
    post:
      operationId: create_return
      summary: Create a return request and decide whether it is auto-approved or escalated to manual review.
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Successful mock response
  /returns/{returnId}:
    get:
      operationId: get_return
      summary: Fetch the current return state after creation has mutated mock runtime state.
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Successful mock response

deployment:
    role: data_plane
    role_data_plane:
        config_provider: yaml
plugins:
    - name: ext-plugin-pre-req
    - name: grpc-transcode
    - name: kafka-proxy
    - name: proxy-rewrite
protos:
    - id: returns-control-plane.quote-refund
      desc: api/proto/refund_pricing.proto
      content: |
        syntax = "proto3";

        package returns_control_plane.v1;

        service refund_pricingService {
          rpc QuoteRefund (QuoteRefundRequest) returns (QuoteRefundResponse);
        }

        message PingRequest {}

        message PingResponse {
          string status = 1;
        }
routes:
    - id: returns-control-plane.create-return
      name: create-return
      desc: Create a return request and decide whether it is auto-approved or escalated to manual review.
      uri: /returns
      methods:
        - POST
      hosts:
        - returns-control-plane.mock.internal
      plugins:
        ext-plugin-pre-req:
            allow_degradation: true
            conf:
                - name: babelsuite-resolver
                  value: '{"compatibility_url":"/mocks/rest/returns-control-plane/returns-api/returns","operation_id":"create-return","protocol":"REST","public_path":"/returns","resolver_url":"/internal/mock-data/returns-control-plane/returns-api/create-return"}'
        proxy-rewrite:
            headers:
                set:
                    X-Babelsuite-Dispatcher: apisix
                    X-Babelsuite-Operation: create-return
            uri: /mocks/rest/returns-control-plane/returns-api/returns
      upstream:
        type: roundrobin
        scheme: http
        nodes:
            ${{BABELSUITE_ENGINE_ADDR}}: 1
    - id: returns-control-plane.get-return
      name: get-return
      desc: Fetch the current return state after creation has mutated mock runtime state.
      uri: /returns/*
      methods:
        - GET
      hosts:
        - returns-control-plane.mock.internal
      plugins:
        ext-plugin-pre-req:
            allow_degradation: true
            conf:
                - name: babelsuite-resolver
                  value: '{"compatibility_url":"/mocks/rest/returns-control-plane/returns-api/returns/ret_1001","operation_id":"get-return","protocol":"REST","public_path":"/returns/{returnId}","resolver_url":"/internal/mock-data/returns-control-plane/returns-api/get-return"}'
        proxy-rewrite:
            headers:
                set:
                    X-Babelsuite-Dispatcher: apisix
                    X-Babelsuite-Operation: get-return
            regex_uri:
                - ^/returns/([^/]+)$
                - /mocks/rest/returns-control-plane/returns-api/returns/$1
      upstream:
        type: roundrobin
        scheme: http
        nodes:
            ${{BABELSUITE_ENGINE_ADDR}}: 1
    - id: returns-control-plane.publish-refund-authorized.kafka
      name: publish-refund-authorized
      desc: Publish a refund-authorized event into the worker lane.
      uri: /returns.refund.authorized
      methods:
        - POST
      hosts:
        - returns-control-plane.mock.internal
      plugins:
        ext-plugin-pre-req:
            allow_degradation: true
            conf:
                - name: babelsuite-resolver
                  value: '{"compatibility_url":"/mocks/async/returns-control-plane/refund-events/publish-refund-authorized","operation_id":"publish-refund-authorized","protocol":"KAFKA","public_path":"/returns.refund.authorized","resolver_url":"/internal/mock-data/returns-control-plane/refund-events/publish-refund-authorized"}'
        kafka-proxy:
            sasl:
                password: ${{BABELSUITE_KAFKA_PASSWORD}}
                username: ${{BABELSUITE_KAFKA_USERNAME}}
      upstream:
        type: roundrobin
        scheme: kafka
        nodes:
            ${{APISIX_KAFKA_UPSTREAM_ADDR}}: 1
    - id: returns-control-plane.quote-refund.grpc
      name: quote-refund
      desc: Quote the refund amount and decide whether the request is low-risk or needs manual review.
      uri: /returns.v1.RefundPricing/QuoteRefund
      methods:
        - POST
      hosts:
        - returns-control-plane.mock.internal
      plugins:
        ext-plugin-pre-req:
            allow_degradation: true
            conf:
                - name: babelsuite-resolver
                  value: '{"compatibility_url":"/mocks/grpc/returns-control-plane/pricing-service/quote-refund","operation_id":"quote-refund","protocol":"GRPC","public_path":"/returns.v1.RefundPricing/QuoteRefund","resolver_url":"/internal/mock-data/returns-control-plane/pricing-service/quote-refund"}'
        grpc-transcode:
            method: QuoteRefund
            proto_id: returns-control-plane.quote-refund
            service: returns.v1.RefundPricing
      upstream:
        type: roundrobin
        scheme: grpc
        nodes:
            ${{APISIX_GRPC_UPSTREAM_ADDR}}: 1

# Set BABELSUITE_ENGINE_ADDR to the in-agent BabelSuite engine endpoint, for example babelsuite-engine:8090.
# HTTP-family routes below keep a proxy-rewrite compatibility path so suites still run while the APISIX sidecar rolls out protocol-specific plugins.
# gRPC and Kafka surfaces now render as live APISIX routes, while MQTT/TCP/UDP surfaces render as live APISIX stream routes and shared upstream objects.
# Query parameters continue to flow to the engine unchanged so mock dispatch still happens in BabelSuite.
#
# Resolver contracts below describe the internal BabelSuite endpoint APISIX should call to fetch normalized mock data:
# - returns-control-plane.create-return (REST) public=/returns resolver=/internal/mock-data/returns-control-plane/returns-api/create-return compatibility=/mocks/rest/returns-control-plane/returns-api/returns
# - returns-control-plane.get-return (REST) public=/returns/{returnId} resolver=/internal/mock-data/returns-control-plane/returns-api/get-return compatibility=/mocks/rest/returns-control-plane/returns-api/returns/ret_1001
# - returns-control-plane.publish-refund-authorized (Kafka) public=/returns.refund.authorized resolver=/internal/mock-data/returns-control-plane/refund-events/publish-refund-authorized compatibility=/mocks/async/returns-control-plane/refund-events/publish-refund-authorized
# - returns-control-plane.quote-refund (gRPC) public=/returns.v1.RefundPricing/QuoteRefund resolver=/internal/mock-data/returns-control-plane/pricing-service/quote-refund compatibility=/mocks/grpc/returns-control-plane/pricing-service/quote-refund
#END

apiVersion: mocks.babelsuite.io/v1alpha1
kind: MockOperation
metadata:
    metadataPath: mock/events/refund-authorized.metadata.yaml
    operationId: publish-refund-authorized
    sourceArtifact: mock/events/refund-authorized.cue
    suite: returns-control-plane
spec:
    adapter: async
    delayMillis: 0
    fallback:
        mode: example
        examplename: authorized-event
        proxyurl: ""
        status: ""
        mediatype: ""
        body: ""
        headers: []
    parameterConstraints: []
    resolverUrl: /internal/mock-data/returns-control-plane/refund-events/publish-refund-authorized
    runtimeUrl: /mocks/async/returns-control-plane/refund-events/publish-refund-authorized
    state: null

apiVersion: mocks.babelsuite.io/v1alpha1
kind: MockOperation
metadata:
    metadataPath: mock/pricing/quote-refund.metadata.yaml
    operationId: quote-refund
    sourceArtifact: mock/pricing/quote-refund.cue
    suite: returns-control-plane
spec:
    adapter: grpc
    delayMillis: 0
    fallback:
        mode: static
        examplename: ""
        proxyurl: ""
        status: "5"
        mediatype: application/json
        body: |-
            {
              "code": "QUOTE_NOT_AVAILABLE",
              "message": "No refund quote mock matched the request."
            }
        headers: []
    parameterConstraints:
        - name: x-risk-tier
          source: header
          required: false
          forward: true
          pattern: ""
    resolverUrl: /internal/mock-data/returns-control-plane/pricing-service/quote-refund
    runtimeUrl: /mocks/grpc/returns-control-plane/pricing-service/quote-refund
    state: null

apiVersion: mocks.babelsuite.io/v1alpha1
kind: MockOperation
metadata:
    metadataPath: mock/returns/create-return.metadata.yaml
    operationId: create-return
    sourceArtifact: mock/returns/create-return.cue
    suite: returns-control-plane
spec:
    adapter: rest
    delayMillis: 120
    fallback:
        mode: static
        examplename: ""
        proxyurl: ""
        status: "422"
        mediatype: application/json
        body: |-
            {
              "code": "UNKNOWN_RETURN_SCENARIO",
              "message": "No return mock matched the incoming scenario."
            }
        headers: []
    parameterConstraints:
        - name: scenario
          source: query
          required: true
          forward: false
          pattern: ^(approved|manual-review)$
        - name: content-type
          source: header
          required: true
          forward: false
          pattern: (?i)^application/json
        - name: x-suite-profile
          source: header
          required: false
          forward: true
          pattern: ""
    resolverUrl: /internal/mock-data/returns-control-plane/returns-api/create-return
    runtimeUrl: /mocks/rest/returns-control-plane/returns-api/returns?scenario=approved
    state:
        lookupkeytemplate: ""
        mutationkeytemplate: return:{{ response.body.returnId }}
        defaults:
            refundMode: pending
            reviewQueue: triage
            status: created
        transitions:
            - onexample: approved-standard
              mutationkeytemplate: ""
              set:
                profile: '{{ request.headers.x-suite-profile }}'
                refundMode: instant_credit
                reviewQueue: auto
                status: approved
              delete: []
              increment: {}
            - onexample: manual-review
              mutationkeytemplate: ""
              set:
                profile: '{{ request.headers.x-suite-profile }}'
                refundMode: agent_review
                reviewQueue: manual
                status: manual_review
              delete: []
              increment: {}

apiVersion: mocks.babelsuite.io/v1alpha1
kind: MockOperation
metadata:
    metadataPath: mock/returns/get-return.metadata.yaml
    operationId: get-return
    sourceArtifact: mock/returns/get-return.cue
    suite: returns-control-plane
spec:
    adapter: rest
    delayMillis: 0
    fallback:
        mode: static
        examplename: ""
        proxyurl: ""
        status: "404"
        mediatype: application/json
        body: |-
            {
              "code": "RETURN_NOT_FOUND",
              "message": "No persisted state exists for {{ request.path.returnId }}."
            }
        headers: []
    parameterConstraints:
        - name: returnId
          source: path
          required: true
          forward: false
          pattern: ^ret_[0-9]+$
        - name: x-request-id
          source: header
          required: false
          forward: true
          pattern: ""
    resolverUrl: /internal/mock-data/returns-control-plane/returns-api/get-return
    runtimeUrl: /mocks/rest/returns-control-plane/returns-api/returns/ret_1001
    state:
        lookupkeytemplate: return:{{ request.path.returnId }}
        mutationkeytemplate: ""
        defaults:
            profile: local.yaml
            refundMode: instant_credit
            reviewQueue: auto
            status: approved
        transitions: []

name: Canary Routing
description: Canary refund rules with manual-review escalation and delayed callbacks.
default: false
runtime:
  suite: returns-control-plane
  repository: localhost:5000/qa/returns-control-plane
  profileFile: canary.yaml
modules:
    - postgres
    - kafka
    - grpc
observability:
  logs: structured
  traces: enabled
  metrics: enabled

name: Local Debug
description: Verbose logs, seeded return ids, and open worker traces.
default: true
runtime:
  suite: returns-control-plane
  repository: localhost:5000/qa/returns-control-plane
  profileFile: local.yaml
modules:
    - postgres
    - kafka
    - grpc
observability:
  logs: structured
  traces: enabled
  metrics: enabled

name: Peak Season
description: High-volume return traffic with fraud screening and worker backpressure.
default: false
runtime:
  suite: returns-control-plane
  repository: localhost:5000/qa/returns-control-plane
  profileFile: peak.yaml
modules:
    - postgres
    - kafka
    - grpc
observability:
  logs: structured
  traces: enabled
  metrics: enabled

const suiteId = "returns-control-plane"
const scriptName = "seed_routes.ts"

console.log(`running ${scriptName} for ${suiteId}`)

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

echo "bootstrapping seed_topics.sh for Returns Control Plane"
echo "resolved modules: postgres, kafka, grpc"

contracts: Examples live in mock/ while runtime behavior is split into adjacent metadata files so dispatch logic can evolve without rewriting payloads.
Return creation writes persisted mock state that the lookup operation renders later through templates.
REST, gRPC, and async surfaces share one mock engine while keeping protocol-specific runtime adapters at the edge.

apiSurfaces.title: Returns API
apiSurfaces.description: REST surface for creating and reading returns with stateful decisions and strict parameter validation.

operations.name: /returns
operations.summary: Create a return request and decide whether it is auto-approved or escalated to manual review.

mockMetadata.dispatcherRules: The APISIX sidecar matches POST /returns and calls /internal/mock-data/returns-control-plane/returns-api/create-return to fetch normalized status, headers, and body while APISIX remains the public responder.

operations.name: /returns/{returnId}
operations.summary: Fetch the current return state after creation has mutated mock runtime state.

mockMetadata.dispatcherRules: The APISIX sidecar matches GET /returns/{returnId} and calls /internal/mock-data/returns-control-plane/returns-api/get-return to fetch normalized status, headers, and body while APISIX remains the public responder.

apiSurfaces.title: Refund Pricing
apiSurfaces.description: gRPC refund-quote surface that reuses the shared mock engine through a thin protocol adapter.

operations.name: returns.v1.RefundPricing/QuoteRefund
operations.summary: Quote the refund amount and decide whether the request is low-risk or needs manual review.

mockMetadata.dispatcherRules: The APISIX sidecar should terminate or normalize gRPC traffic for /returns.v1.RefundPricing/QuoteRefund and call /internal/mock-data/returns-control-plane/pricing-service/quote-refund to fetch normalized status, headers, and body so APISIX remains the visible responder while BabelSuite only resolves the matched exchange.

apiSurfaces.title: Refund Events
apiSurfaces.description: Async event surface for refund authorization callbacks and worker validation flows.

operations.name: returns.refund.authorized
operations.summary: Publish a refund-authorized event into the worker lane.

mockMetadata.dispatcherRules: The APISIX sidecar should use a plugin-backed event adapter for /returns.refund.authorized and call /internal/mock-data/returns-control-plane/refund-events/publish-refund-authorized to fetch normalized payload data so APISIX owns the broker-facing response flow while BabelSuite only generates the mock payload.

title: SOAP Claims Hub
description: Legacy claims sandbox with a SOAP intake surface, XML envelopes, and APISIX-fronted dispatch into the BabelSuite mock engine.
suiteStar:
load("@babelsuite/runtime", "service", "task", "test")

claims_mock = service.mock()
seed_reference_data = task.run(file="seed_reference_data.sh", image="bash:5.2", after=[claims_mock])
claims_bridge = service.run(after=[claims_mock, seed_reference_data])
claims_smoke = test.run(file="claims_smoke.py", image="python:3.12", after=[claims_bridge])

profiles.label: Local Debug
profiles.description: Relaxed SOAP timeouts with verbose envelope logging.

profiles.label: Canary Partner
profiles.description: Partner-like SOAP headers and stricter claim review toggles.

folders.name: profiles
folders.description: SOAP endpoint runtime knobs and partner header defaults.

folders.name: api
folders.description: WSDL contract published to legacy claim submitters.

folders.name: mock
folders.description: Schema-driven SOAP mock definitions that render XML envelopes at runtime.

folders.name: tasks
folders.description: Bootstrap hooks for claim code tables and partner fixtures.

folders.name: tests
folders.description: Smoke coverage for submit and lookup SOAP exchanges.

folders.name: fixtures
folders.description: Seeded partner claims and policy fixtures.

folders.name: policies
folders.description: SOAP fault and envelope validation policies.

folders.name: gateway
folders.description: Runtime-managed APISIX sidecar routes that front suite APIs and forward traffic into the BabelSuite mock engine.

sourceFiles.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<definitions xmlns="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/wsdl/" xmlns:soap="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/wsdl/soap/" xmlns:tns="urn:babelsuite:soap-claims-hub" xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" name="claimsService" targetNamespace="urn:babelsuite:soap-claims-hub">
  <types>
    <xsd:schema targetNamespace="urn:babelsuite:soap-claims-hub">
      <xsd:element name="SubmitClaimRequest" type="xsd:string"/>
      <xsd:element name="SubmitClaimResponse" type="xsd:string"/>
      <xsd:element name="GetClaimStatusRequest" type="xsd:string"/>
      <xsd:element name="GetClaimStatusResponse" type="xsd:string"/>
    </xsd:schema>
  </types>

  <message name="SubmitClaimInput">
    <part name="parameters" element="tns:SubmitClaimRequest"/>
  </message>
  <message name="SubmitClaimOutput">
    <part name="parameters" element="tns:SubmitClaimResponse"/>
  </message>

  <message name="GetClaimStatusInput">
    <part name="parameters" element="tns:GetClaimStatusRequest"/>
  </message>
  <message name="GetClaimStatusOutput">
    <part name="parameters" element="tns:GetClaimStatusResponse"/>
  </message>

  <portType name="claimsPortType">
    <operation name="SubmitClaim">
      <input message="tns:SubmitClaimInput"/>
      <output message="tns:SubmitClaimOutput"/>
    </operation>
    <operation name="GetClaimStatus">
      <input message="tns:GetClaimStatusInput"/>
      <output message="tns:GetClaimStatusOutput"/>
    </operation>
  </portType>
  <binding name="claimsBinding" type="tns:claimsPortType">
    <soap:binding transport="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/http" style="document"/>
    <operation name="SubmitClaim">
      <soap:operation soapAction="urn:SubmitClaim"/>
      <input><soap:body use="literal"/></input>
      <output><soap:body use="literal"/></output>
    </operation>
    <operation name="GetClaimStatus">
      <soap:operation soapAction="urn:GetClaimStatus"/>
      <input><soap:body use="literal"/></input>
      <output><soap:body use="literal"/></output>
    </operation>
  </binding>
  <service name="claimsService">
    <port name="claimsPort" binding="tns:claimsBinding">
      <soap:address location="https://soap-claims-hub.mock.internal/ClaimService"/>
    </port>
  </service>
</definitions>

deployment:
    role: data_plane
    role_data_plane:
        config_provider: yaml
plugins:
    - name: ext-plugin-pre-req
    - name: proxy-rewrite
routes:
    - id: soap-claims-hub.claim-service
      name: claim-service
      desc: Accept SubmitClaim and GetClaimStatus SOAP actions on the shared claims endpoint.
      uri: /ClaimService
      methods:
        - POST
      hosts:
        - soap-claims-hub.mock.internal
      plugins:
        ext-plugin-pre-req:
            allow_degradation: true
            conf:
                - name: babelsuite-resolver
                  value: '{"compatibility_url":"/mocks/rest/soap-claims-hub/claims-soap/ClaimService","operation_id":"claim-service","protocol":"SOAP","public_path":"/ClaimService","resolver_url":"/internal/mock-data/soap-claims-hub/claims-soap/claim-service"}'
        proxy-rewrite:
            headers:
                set:
                    X-Babelsuite-Dispatcher: apisix
                    X-Babelsuite-Operation: claim-service
            uri: /mocks/rest/soap-claims-hub/claims-soap/ClaimService
      upstream:
        type: roundrobin
        scheme: http
        nodes:
            ${{BABELSUITE_ENGINE_ADDR}}: 1

# Set BABELSUITE_ENGINE_ADDR to the in-agent BabelSuite engine endpoint, for example babelsuite-engine:8090.
# HTTP-family routes below keep a proxy-rewrite compatibility path so suites still run while the APISIX sidecar rolls out protocol-specific plugins.
# gRPC and Kafka surfaces now render as live APISIX routes, while MQTT/TCP/UDP surfaces render as live APISIX stream routes and shared upstream objects.
# Query parameters continue to flow to the engine unchanged so mock dispatch still happens in BabelSuite.
#
# Resolver contracts below describe the internal BabelSuite endpoint APISIX should call to fetch normalized mock data:
# - soap-claims-hub.claim-service (SOAP) public=/ClaimService resolver=/internal/mock-data/soap-claims-hub/claims-soap/claim-service compatibility=/mocks/rest/soap-claims-hub/claims-soap/ClaimService
#END

apiVersion: mocks.babelsuite.io/v1alpha1
kind: MockOperation
metadata:
    metadataPath: mock/claims/claim-service.metadata.yaml
    operationId: claim-service
    sourceArtifact: mock/claims/claim-service.cue
    suite: soap-claims-hub
spec:
    adapter: rest
    delayMillis: 90
    fallback:
        mode: static
        examplename: ""
        proxyurl: ""
        status: "500"
        mediatype: text/xml; charset=utf-8
        body: |-
            <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
            <soapenv:Envelope xmlns:soapenv="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/">
              <soapenv:Body>
                <soapenv:Fault>
                  <faultcode>soapenv:Client</faultcode>
                  <faultstring>No SOAPAction matched the incoming request.</faultstring>
                </soapenv:Fault>
              </soapenv:Body>
            </soapenv:Envelope>
        headers: []
    parameterConstraints:
        - name: content-type
          source: header
          required: true
          forward: false
          pattern: (?i)^(text/xml|application/soap\+xml)
        - name: soapaction
          source: header
          required: true
          forward: false
          pattern: ^urn:(SubmitClaim|GetClaimStatus)$
        - name: x-suite-profile
          source: header
          required: false
          forward: true
          pattern: ""
    resolverUrl: /internal/mock-data/soap-claims-hub/claims-soap/claim-service
    runtimeUrl: /mocks/rest/soap-claims-hub/claims-soap/ClaimService
    state: null

name: Canary Partner
description: Partner-like SOAP headers and stricter claim review toggles.
default: false
runtime:
  suite: soap-claims-hub
  repository: localhost:5000/enterprise-integration/soap-claims-hub
  profileFile: canary.yaml
modules:
    - runtime
observability:
  logs: structured
  traces: enabled
  metrics: enabled

name: Local Debug
description: Relaxed SOAP timeouts with verbose envelope logging.
default: true
runtime:
  suite: soap-claims-hub
  repository: localhost:5000/enterprise-integration/soap-claims-hub
  profileFile: local.yaml
modules:
    - runtime
observability:
  logs: structured
  traces: enabled
  metrics: enabled

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

echo "bootstrapping seed_reference_data.sh for SOAP Claims Hub"
echo "resolved modules: "

contracts: The SOAP endpoint is fronted by APISIX, but BabelSuite still owns the request/response mock generation behind the sidecar.
WSDL stays under api/ while schema-backed mock definitions in mock/ drive runtime XML envelopes.
SOAPAction is treated as the primary dispatch key so legacy XML clients do not need JSON-specific adapters.

apiSurfaces.title: Claims SOAP Service
apiSurfaces.description: SOAP 1.1 claim intake and status lookup routed through APISIX into the shared BabelSuite mock engine.

operations.name: /ClaimService
operations.summary: Accept SubmitClaim and GetClaimStatus SOAP actions on the shared claims endpoint.
operations.curlCommand:
curl -X POST "https://soap-claims-hub.mock.internal/ClaimService" -H "content-type: text/xml; charset=utf-8" -H "soapaction: urn:SubmitClaim" -H "x-suite-profile: canary.yaml" -d '<soapenv:Envelope xmlns:soapenv="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/" xmlns:clm="urn:claims:v1">
  <soapenv:Header/>
  <soapenv:Body>
    <clm:SubmitClaimRequest>
      <clm:PolicyNumber>PL-1024</clm:PolicyNumber>
      <clm:LossType>water</clm:LossType>
      <clm:Amount>4200</clm:Amount>
    </clm:SubmitClaimRequest>
  </soapenv:Body>
</soapenv:Envelope>'

mockMetadata.dispatcherRules: The APISIX sidecar should terminate SOAP envelopes on POST /ClaimService, preserve SOAPAction routing, and call /internal/mock-data/soap-claims-hub/claims-soap/claim-service to fetch the matched XML response while APISIX remains the public SOAP responder.

fallback.body:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<soapenv:Envelope xmlns:soapenv="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/">
  <soapenv:Body>
    <soapenv:Fault>
      <faultcode>soapenv:Client</faultcode>
      <faultstring>No SOAPAction matched the incoming request.</faultstring>
    </soapenv:Fault>
  </soapenv:Body>
</soapenv:Envelope>

title: Storefront Browser Lab
description: Browser-first commerce lab with Kafka event streams, Playwright checkout journeys, and mock APIs for promo traffic validation.
suiteStar:
load("@babelsuite/kafka", "kafka")
load("@babelsuite/playwright", "playwright")
load("@babelsuite/runtime", "service", "task", "test")

broker = service.run()
catalog_mock = service.mock(after=[broker])
orders_mock = service.mock(after=[broker])
seed_topics = task.run(file="seed_topics.sh", image="bash:5.2", after=[broker])
event_consumer = service.run(after=[broker, seed_topics, orders_mock])
storefront_api = service.run(after=[catalog_mock, orders_mock, seed_topics])
storefront_ui = service.run(after=[storefront_api])
playwright_checkout = test.run(file="playwright_checkout.spec.ts", image="mcr.microsoft.com/playwright:v1.53.0-noble", after=[storefront_ui, event_consumer])

profiles.label: Local Debug
profiles.description: Opens browser traces, seeded demo users, and single-worker Kafka consumption.

profiles.label: CI Browser
profiles.description: Headless Playwright suite with tighter timeouts and deterministic mocks.

profiles.label: Promo Burst
profiles.description: High-throughput cart and checkout traffic with promotional catalog fixtures.

folders.name: profiles
folders.description: Browser, Kafka, and mock dispatch settings for local, CI, and campaign traffic.

folders.name: api
folders.description: Order and catalog contracts exposed to the UI and background consumer.

folders.name: mock
folders.description: Mock API payloads for product catalog and order submission paths.

folders.name: tasks
folders.description: Kafka bootstrap and browser fixture warm-up hooks.

folders.name: tests
folders.description: Playwright coverage for checkout success and cart abandonment journeys.

folders.name: fixtures
folders.description: Seeded products, campaigns, and browser-side user sessions.

folders.name: policies
folders.description: Event schema and checkout latency validation rules.

folders.name: gateway
folders.description: Runtime-managed APISIX sidecar routes that front suite APIs and forward traffic into the BabelSuite mock engine.

sourceFiles.content:
openapi: 3.1.0
info:
  title: Storefront Browser Lab
  version: v1.3.0
servers:
  - url: https://storefront-browser-lab.mock.internal
paths:
  /catalog/products:
    get:
      operationId: list_products
      summary: Return the product grid shown to the storefront browser before checkout begins.
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Successful mock response
  /orders:
    post:
      operationId: create_order
      summary: Submit a checkout order and emit a Kafka event consumed by the browser verification lane.
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Successful mock response

deployment:
    role: data_plane
    role_data_plane:
        config_provider: yaml
plugins:
    - name: ext-plugin-pre-req
    - name: proxy-rewrite
routes:
    - id: storefront-browser-lab.create-order
      name: create-order
      desc: Submit a checkout order and emit a Kafka event consumed by the browser verification lane.
      uri: /orders
      methods:
        - POST
      hosts:
        - storefront-browser-lab.mock.internal
      plugins:
        ext-plugin-pre-req:
            allow_degradation: true
            conf:
                - name: babelsuite-resolver
                  value: '{"compatibility_url":"/mocks/rest/storefront-browser-lab/storefront-api/orders","operation_id":"create-order","protocol":"REST","public_path":"/orders","resolver_url":"/internal/mock-data/storefront-browser-lab/storefront-api/create-order"}'
        proxy-rewrite:
            headers:
                set:
                    X-Babelsuite-Dispatcher: apisix
                    X-Babelsuite-Operation: create-order
            uri: /mocks/rest/storefront-browser-lab/storefront-api/orders
      upstream:
        type: roundrobin
        scheme: http
        nodes:
            ${{BABELSUITE_ENGINE_ADDR}}: 1
    - id: storefront-browser-lab.list-products
      name: list-products
      desc: Return the product grid shown to the storefront browser before checkout begins.
      uri: /catalog/products
      methods:
        - GET
      hosts:
        - storefront-browser-lab.mock.internal
      plugins:
        ext-plugin-pre-req:
            allow_degradation: true
            conf:
                - name: babelsuite-resolver
                  value: '{"compatibility_url":"/mocks/rest/storefront-browser-lab/storefront-api/catalog/products","operation_id":"list-products","protocol":"REST","public_path":"/catalog/products","resolver_url":"/internal/mock-data/storefront-browser-lab/storefront-api/list-products"}'
        proxy-rewrite:
            headers:
                set:
                    X-Babelsuite-Dispatcher: apisix
                    X-Babelsuite-Operation: list-products
            uri: /mocks/rest/storefront-browser-lab/storefront-api/catalog/products
      upstream:
        type: roundrobin
        scheme: http
        nodes:
            ${{BABELSUITE_ENGINE_ADDR}}: 1

# Set BABELSUITE_ENGINE_ADDR to the in-agent BabelSuite engine endpoint, for example babelsuite-engine:8090.
# HTTP-family routes below keep a proxy-rewrite compatibility path so suites still run while the APISIX sidecar rolls out protocol-specific plugins.
# gRPC and Kafka surfaces now render as live APISIX routes, while MQTT/TCP/UDP surfaces render as live APISIX stream routes and shared upstream objects.
# Query parameters continue to flow to the engine unchanged so mock dispatch still happens in BabelSuite.
#
# Resolver contracts below describe the internal BabelSuite endpoint APISIX should call to fetch normalized mock data:
# - storefront-browser-lab.create-order (REST) public=/orders resolver=/internal/mock-data/storefront-browser-lab/storefront-api/create-order compatibility=/mocks/rest/storefront-browser-lab/storefront-api/orders
# - storefront-browser-lab.list-products (REST) public=/catalog/products resolver=/internal/mock-data/storefront-browser-lab/storefront-api/list-products compatibility=/mocks/rest/storefront-browser-lab/storefront-api/catalog/products
#END

apiVersion: mocks.babelsuite.io/v1alpha1
kind: MockOperation
metadata:
    metadataPath: mock/catalog/list-products.metadata.yaml
    operationId: list-products
    sourceArtifact: mock/catalog/list-products.cue
    suite: storefront-browser-lab
spec:
    adapter: rest
    delayMillis: 0
    fallback:
        mode: static
        examplename: ""
        proxyurl: ""
        status: "200"
        mediatype: application/json
        body: |-
            {
              "products": [],
              "campaign": "default"
            }
        headers:
            - name: x-mock-fallback
              value: catalog-empty
    parameterConstraints:
        - name: scenario
          source: query
          required: true
          forward: false
          pattern: ^(promo)$
        - name: accept
          source: header
          required: true
          forward: false
          pattern: (?i)application/json
    resolverUrl: /internal/mock-data/storefront-browser-lab/storefront-api/list-products
    runtimeUrl: /mocks/rest/storefront-browser-lab/storefront-api/catalog/products?scenario=promo
    state: null

apiVersion: mocks.babelsuite.io/v1alpha1
kind: MockOperation
metadata:
    metadataPath: mock/orders/create-order.metadata.yaml
    operationId: create-order
    sourceArtifact: mock/orders/create-order.cue
    suite: storefront-browser-lab
spec:
    adapter: rest
    delayMillis: 0
    fallback:
        mode: static
        examplename: ""
        proxyurl: ""
        status: "422"
        mediatype: application/json
        body: |-
            {
              "code": "UNKNOWN_ORDER_SCENARIO",
              "message": "No order mock matched the incoming scenario."
            }
        headers: []
    parameterConstraints:
        - name: scenario
          source: query
          required: true
          forward: false
          pattern: ^(happy-path|out-of-stock)$
        - name: content-type
          source: header
          required: true
          forward: false
          pattern: (?i)^application/json
        - name: x-browser-suite
          source: header
          required: false
          forward: true
          pattern: ""
    resolverUrl: /internal/mock-data/storefront-browser-lab/storefront-api/create-order
    runtimeUrl: /mocks/rest/storefront-browser-lab/storefront-api/orders?scenario=happy-path
    state:
        lookupkeytemplate: checkout-attempt:{{ request.body.email }}
        mutationkeytemplate: checkout-attempt:{{ request.body.email }}
        defaults:
            count: "0"
        transitions:
            - onexample: happy-path
              mutationkeytemplate: ""
              set:
                lastOrderId: '{{ response.body.orderId }}'
                lastStatus: accepted
              delete: []
              increment:
                count: 1
            - onexample: out-of-stock
              mutationkeytemplate: ""
              set:
                lastStatus: rejected
                reason: inventory
              delete: []
              increment:
                count: 1

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

echo "bootstrapping seed_topics.sh for Storefront Browser Lab"
echo "resolved modules: kafka, playwright"

const suiteId = "storefront-browser-lab"
const scriptName = "warm_cache.ts"

console.log(`running ${scriptName} for ${suiteId}`)

contracts: Playwright scenarios consume strict base URLs from suite outputs rather than hard-coded localhost ports.
Kafka publishes checkout and cart events through a fixed topic contract so browser assertions can wait on stable signals.
Mock APIs under mock/ provide deterministic catalog and order responses without changing the immutable api/ contracts.

apiSurfaces.title: Storefront Orders API
apiSurfaces.description: Catalog and order endpoints served through deterministic mock APIs so Playwright can validate browser flows without backend drift.

operations.name: /catalog/products
operations.summary: Return the product grid shown to the storefront browser before checkout begins.

mockMetadata.dispatcherRules: The APISIX sidecar matches GET /catalog/products and calls /internal/mock-data/storefront-browser-lab/storefront-api/list-products to fetch normalized status, headers, and body while APISIX remains the public responder.

operations.name: /orders
operations.summary: Submit a checkout order and emit a Kafka event consumed by the browser verification lane.

mockMetadata.dispatcherRules: The APISIX sidecar matches POST /orders and calls /internal/mock-data/storefront-browser-lab/storefront-api/create-order to fetch normalized status, headers, and body while APISIX remains the public responder.
